prerequisites: A running PostgreSQL server reachable at a known host and port (default 5432).
An existing target database (the 'database' field) and a schema to write into (the 'schema' field).

A PostgreSQL role/user (the 'username' field) that can connect to the database and create/write tables in the target schema. Use CREATE ROLE / GRANT to provision privileges as described in the Database Roles and Privileges docs.
Network access to the server: either direct connectivity, or an SSH bastion host if using the optional 'tunnel_method' (SSH key or SSH password).
For encrypted connections, the appropriate SSL/TLS material: a CA certificate and, for client-certificate verification, a client certificate and key (the 'ssl_mode' fields).

authMethods.name: Username and password authentication
authMethods.summary: Authenticate the configured 'username' with a 'password' against the PostgreSQL server. PostgreSQL stores and checks passwords using password-based methods such as scram-sha-256 (recommended) or md5, selected by the server's pg_hba.conf and password_encryption setting.

authMethods.name: SSL/TLS connection with optional client certificate
authMethods.summary: Encrypt the connection via the 'ssl' / 'ssl_mode' settings. Modes such as verify-ca and verify-full validate the server using a supplied CA certificate (ssl_mode.ca_certificate); client-certificate authentication additionally presents a client certificate and key (ssl_mode.client_certificate, ssl_mode.client_key, with optional ssl_mode.client_key_password) as described in libpq SSL Support.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel access
authMethods.summary: Optionally reach a PostgreSQL server that is not directly accessible by tunneling through an SSH bastion via 'tunnel_method', authenticating to the bastion with either an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or a password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). This secures network transport and is separate from PostgreSQL's own user authentication.

setupSteps.title: Provision a database, schema, and role
setupSteps.body: On the PostgreSQL server, ensure the target database exists and create a role for the connector with CREATE ROLE ... LOGIN PASSWORD '...'. Grant the role the privileges it needs to connect and to create and write tables in the target schema (see Database Roles and Privileges and Role Attributes). Record the username and password for the connector config.

setupSteps.title: Configure server authentication (pg_hba.conf)
setupSteps.body: Confirm the server's client authentication (pg_hba.conf) permits the connector's host/network to connect to the chosen database with a password method (scram-sha-256 or md5). See Client Authentication and Password Authentication in the PostgreSQL docs.

setupSteps.title: Enter connection details
setupSteps.body: Provide the required fields: host, port (default 5432), database, schema, and username. Supply the password unless authentication is handled another way. Optionally set jdbc_url_params for extra JDBC connection parameters.

setupSteps.title: Configure SSL/TLS (optional)
setupSteps.body: If the server requires or supports encryption, enable 'ssl' and choose an 'ssl_mode'. For verify-ca / verify-full provide the CA certificate; for client-certificate authentication also provide the client certificate, client key, and (if the key is encrypted) the client key password.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel (optional)
setupSteps.body: If the database is only reachable through a bastion, set 'tunnel_method' to SSH and authenticate to the bastion with either an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or a tunnel user password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password).
